pr.title: gr Shrineroute and Kinokoroute matches, function renaming
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Stage-focused matching PR that implemented two Shrine Route functions, partially advanced Kinoko Route, and performed broad semantic renames of common GObj helpers. decomp.dev reported matched code rising to 57.96% (+0.01%, +316 bytes), with `grShrineRoute_8020AD58` and `grShrineRoute_8020B020` newly 100% matched; `grKinokoRoute_8020754C` improved to 99.97% and Kinoko route `.data` to 33.47%. The diff also renamed `Ground_801C14D0` to `Ground_GetStageGObj` and `HSD_GObjProc_8038FD54` to `HSD_GObj_SetupProc` across symbols, headers, implementations, and many call sites.
what_changed: Replaced `/// #grShrineRoute_8020AD58` with a typed implementation taking `Ground*`, `CollData*`, and integer parameters; it checks `CollData.x34_flags.b1234`, uses `ftLib_80086BE0`, `Player_GetEntity`, `Player_GetEntityAtIndex`, and `ftLib_8008731C`, and sets `gp->gv.shrineroute.xCA` / `xCC`.

Replaced `/// #grShrineRoute_8020B020` with a hide/show routine that iterates six route-related `Ground` records, skips `r4 - 189` unless `r4 == -1`, and toggles `JOBJ_HIDDEN` via `HSD_JObjSetFlagsAll` / `HSD_JObjClearFlagsAll` through `gp->gv.shrineroute2.x108`.
Refined `struct grShrineroute_GroundVars2` in `src/melee/gr/types.h` by splitting padding and adding `HSD_GObj* x108` at the needed offset before `x118`.

Added `StageCallbacks grNKr_803E57F0[4]` and implemented `grKinokoRoute_8020754C` as a standard stage GObj setup routine using `Ground_GetStageGObj`, `GObj_SetupGXLink(..., grDisplay_801C5DB0, 3, 0)`, callback0/callback2/callback3 wiring, and OSReport failure handling.
Changed `grKinokoRoute_8020754C`'s header prototype from `void` return to `HSD_GObj*`; changed `grShrineRoute_8020AD58` from `UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS` to explicit parameter types.
Renamed `Ground_801C14D0` to `Ground_GetStageGObj` in `config/GALE01/symbols.txt`, `ground.c`, `ground.h`, and many stage constructor call sites.

Renamed `HSD_GObjProc_8038FD54` to `HSD_GObj_SetupProc` in `config/GALE01/symbols.txt`, baselib `gobjproc.c/h`, and broad call sites across camera, effects, fighters, game modes, stages, items, menus, interface, and VI files.

Annotated `grBb_Route_803E623C` as `data:string` and changed `grBigBlueRoute_8020C238` assert usage from address-named extern strings to `__FILE__` and the literal `gp->u.car.car_info`; this is evidenced in the diff but was not discussed in PR text.
A large fraction of the 191 changed files are mechanical rename, include-order, wrapping, or formatting churn rather than new logic.
smart_moves: The Kinoko route work reused the project-wide stage callback setup idiom: indexed `StageCallbacks`, `Ground_GetStageGObj`, clearing `x8_callback`/`xC_callback`, installing `x1C_callback`, calling callback0 immediately, and scheduling callback2 at priority 4.

The Shrine Route hide/show match was made cleaner by naming the `x108` GObj field in the stage-specific ground-vars struct before using it, instead of leaving a raw padding access pattern hidden in code.
The symbol renames were synchronized across `symbols.txt`, declarations, definitions, and call sites in the same PR, reducing the risk of a mixed numeric-name/semantic-name codebase.

The completed Shrine functions preserved matching-sensitive control flow and stack-shaping details such as `PAD_STACK(0x10)`, nested entity checks, early return after `ftLib_8008731C`, and the unusual `gp = (Ground*) &gp->gobj` iteration form.

The PR made broad semantic names for two heavily reused helpers, which improves future review/search: `Ground_GetStageGObj` describes the stage GObj helper, and `HSD_GObj_SetupProc` describes a proc attachment helper taking a GObj, event callback, and priority.
decomp_lessons: When a stage route constructor is still unknown, compare it against other `StageCallbacks` constructors: most follow the same order of GObj acquisition, Ground callback clearing, GX link setup, callback3 storage, callback0 call, callback2 process setup, and OSReport-on-null.
Expose typed fields inside stage-specific ground-vars structs as soon as a function proves an offset's use; here `HSD_GObj* x108` enabled readable Shrine Route JObj hiding logic.
Do not overclaim near matches: the PR title mentions Kinoko matches, but decomp.dev only reports `grKinokoRoute_8020754C` at 99.97%, while the two Shrine Route functions reached 100%.
Semantic helper renames should be performed atomically across map symbols, headers, implementation, comments, and all call sites; otherwise future matching diffs become noisy and hard to search.

Known assert strings can sometimes replace address-named extern rodata placeholders; this PR changed a Big Blue Route assert to use `__FILE__` and a literal string while marking the backing symbol as `data:string`.

Large style/formatting sweeps can hide the important matching changes; future reviewers should isolate the actual logic changes in `grshrineroute.c`, `grkinokoroute.c`, `gr/types.h`, `ground.*`, and `gobjproc.*` first.
naming_conventions: `Ground_801C14D0` became `Ground_GetStageGObj`: subsystem prefix + action + object, replacing the address suffix once behavior was understood.
`HSD_GObjProc_8038FD54` became `HSD_GObj_SetupProc`: keeps the HSD/GObj namespace and names the operation instead of the address.
Unmatched or not-yet-understood route functions retained the existing stage-prefix/address pattern, e.g. `grShrineRoute_8020AE08` and `grShrineRoute_8020AF38` stayed as address-suffixed placeholders.
Stage callback data kept the stage abbreviation plus address convention, e.g. `grNKr_803E57F0`, while gaining the concrete `StageCallbacks` type.
Headers moved away from `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` where evidence was strong, using explicit `HSD_GObj*`, `Ground*`, and `CollData*` signatures.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: `grShrineRoute_8020AD58` includes `PAD_STACK(0x10)`, suggesting stack padding was needed for the 100% match.
`grShrineRoute_8020B020` keeps the compare shape `comp = r4 - 189` and `if (r4 == -1 || i != comp)` before toggling hidden flags, rather than simplifying the condition.

`grShrineRoute_8020B020` advances through related `Ground` storage with `gp = (Ground*) &gp->gobj`; this odd-looking pointer progression is likely matching-sensitive and should not be casually rewritten.
The Kinoko constructor preserves the standard callback setup order and uses `HSD_GObj_SetupProc(gobj, callbacks->callback2, 4)` only after callback0/callback3 handling.
Project-wide rename from `HSD_GObjProc_8038FD54` to `HSD_GObj_SetupProc` is source-only but improves grepability for all GObj process scheduling sites and their priority values.
review_feedback: No inline review comments were present.
decomp-dev[bot] reported GALE01 matched code 57.96% (+0.01%, +316 bytes), with two new full matches: `grShrineRoute_8020AD58` (+176 bytes) and `grShrineRoute_8020B020` (+140 bytes).
decomp-dev[bot] also reported two partial improvements: `grKinokoRoute_8020754C` (+231 bytes, 0.00% to 99.97%) and `main/melee/gr/grkinokoroute` `.data` (+80 bytes, 0.00% to 33.47%).
There was one empty dismissed review by `ribbanya`; no actionable human feedback or reviewer rationale is available in the slice.
follow_up_queries: Why does `grKinokoRoute_8020754C` remain at 99.97% after this PR, and what small codegen/detail prevents 100%?
Search remaining stage route constructors for the same `StageCallbacks` setup pattern used by `grKinokoRoute_8020754C`.
Audit stage-specific ground-vars structs for padding that should become typed GObj/JObj/LObj fields like `grShrineroute_GroundVars2.x108`.
Check downstream branches or old workspaces for lingering `Ground_801C14D0` and `HSD_GObjProc_8038FD54` names.
When cherry-picking, separate the semantic stage/proc renames from large formatting-only hunks in `gm_18A5.c`, `groldyoshi.c`, `grzebes.c`, and `tyfigupon.c`.
